steps.title: Identify the contextual signal
steps.content: Rising prices had a "counterintuitive" effect: more buyers entered the market expecting further price rises. So rising prices brought out or generated additional demand — the opposite of what economic intuition would predict.

steps.title: Match the precise word
steps.content:
The blank must mean "draw out or generate" — rising prices called forth new demand rather than reducing it. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — satisfying: fulfilling or meeting — satisfying demand means meeting existing demand, not generating new buyers who weren't previously interested. ✗
  • Choice B — capitalizing: taking advantage of — this describes what buyers do with an opportunity, not what rising prices did to demand. ✗
  • Choice C — exploiting: using for gain — like capitalizing, this describes buyers' behavior, not rising prices drawing new buyers into the market. ✗
  • Choice D — eliciting: drawing forth or calling out — rising prices elicited (brought out) new demand from buyers who hadn't previously wanted the toys. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
